text:
New trends in hybrid warfare, such as the Special Military Operation, and multi-domain modern forms of warfare make medical support in the armed forces radically different. On the basis of literature review of publications from PubMed and sources of NATO, this review looks into the most important factors that determine the effectiveness of medical support in new forms of warfare. The main factor is readiness of personnel (the number of medical personnel, their professional competence in relation to warpathologies and minimization of the atrophying of their skills in peacetime), which is in many respects more important than the availability of medical facilities and equipment. Another important factor is the ability of the medical structure to withstand the attack and the resources of the area (whether it is the territory of the country that launches the attack or the territory of the enemy), and in particular local resources on the enemy's territory or hostile territory.

The study based on recent field medical statistics of modern warfare casualties shows that more than 60-80% of casualties are hit by explosive agents of land mines and more than 20-50% of modern warfare casualties are suffered from traumatic brain injury. The number of fatalities occurring immediately after the injury is due to mine-blast effect or brain injury. However, the source of disability in modern warfare casualties, due to more number of injuries suffered by the extremities, which disables the casualty for a longer time. Thus, in spite of being less fatal, the social cost for extremity injuries is more than that of many long-term diseases. Hence, the evacuation from the area of casualty, application of tourniquet for the cessation of bleeding and blood transfusion prior to reaching the medical facility are of paramount importance in relation to management of casualties. However, there is still no consensus on the location or level of casualty that should undergo specialised surgical care in current medical doctrines of modern warfare, and this appears to be not be optimal against the backdrop of the increasingly dynamic, flexible and unpredictable battlefield environment that has to contend with a growing array of symmetric hybrid threats.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
